value.found_quote: Antigravity is architected as an agent-first platform, meaning the Agent Manager (Mission Control) automatically delegates specific tasks to specialized Sub-agents to optimize performance and context usage. For example, when a task requires web research or documentation lookup, the system invokes the Browser Subagent; for command-line operations, it uses the Terminal Subagent.
value.rationale: The page is a Google AI Developers Forum thread where a Google representative describes Antigravity's existing built-in sub-agents (Browser Subagent, Terminal Subagent) delegated by the Agent Manager/Mission Control. This substantiates that Google's Antigravity has sub-agents. Note: the claim's framing about user-creatable custom subagents and 'AgentKit 2.0 / 16 specialized agents' is NOT supported — the thread is largely a feature request for custom user-defined sub-agents, which the page indicates is still under review and not yet available. But the named offering 'Antigravity subagents' is described as present on the page.
